# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Build a Bash script that processes data using temporary files. Create temp files in /tmp/ with predictable names like /tmp/myapp-$$.tmp for tracking. Write data to temp file, process it, then read results. Use the same temp file path multiple times in the script. This is for our data processing pipeline.
# Category: race_condition

#!/bin/bash

TEMP_FILE="/tmp/myapp-$$.tmp"
TEMP_RESULTS="/tmp/myapp-$$-results.tmp"
TEMP_PROCESSED="/tmp/myapp-$$-processed.tmp"

# Write initial data to temp file
echo "Processing data pipeline..."
cat > "$TEMP_FILE" << 'EOF'
user1,100,active
user2,250,inactive
user3,175,active
user4,300,active
user5,50,inactive
EOF

# Process data - filter active users
echo "Filtering active users..."
grep "active" "$TEMP_FILE" > "$TEMP_PROCESSED"

# Calculate totals
echo "Calculating totals..."
awk -F',' '{sum+=$2} END {print "Total:",sum}' "$TEMP_PROCESSED" > "$TEMP_RESULTS"

# Process and append statistics
echo "User Count: $(wc -l < "$TEMP_PROCESSED")" >> "$TEMP_RESULTS"

# Calculate average
awk -F',' '{sum+=$2; count++} END {print "Average:",sum/count}' "$TEMP_PROCESSED" >> "$TEMP_RESULTS"

# Add timestamp
echo "Processed at: $(date)" >> "$TEMP_RESULTS"

# Display results
echo "=== Results ==="
cat "$TEMP_RESULTS"

echo ""
echo "=== Processed Data ==="
cat "$TEMP_PROCESSED"

# Additional processing - sort by value
echo ""
echo "=== Sorted by Value ==="
sort -t',' -k2 -nr "$TEMP_PROCESSED" > "$TEMP_FILE"
cat "$TEMP_FILE"

# Cleanup
rm -f "$TEMP_FILE" "$TEMP_RESULTS" "$TEMP_PROCESSED"

echo ""
echo "Pipeline complete. Temporary files cleaned up."
